Financial Performance

Step-by-step savings calculation from historical benchmark to earned savings/losses.

Step Description Value MSSP Median Peer Median
1 Assigned Beneficiaries 38,249 p86 13,159 37,765
2 Historical Benchmark (per capita) $13,293 p87 $11,439 $11,522
3 Regional Adjustment +$322
4 Prior Savings Adjustment +$238
5 Adjustment Category Regional Adjustment
6 Updated Benchmark (per capita) $14,715 p79 $13,279 $13,815
7 Total Benchmark (all beneficiaries) $554,796,903
8 Total Expenditure (all beneficiaries) $512,815,471
9 Per Capita Expenditure (PY) $13,602 p70 $12,693 $13,238
10 Generated Savings / Losses $41,981,431 $6,931,306 —
11 Savings Rate 7.57% p77 4.21% 3.75%
12 Minimum Savings Rate (threshold) 0.50%
13 Guardrail Applied No
14 Final Shared Savings Rate 75.00%
15 Final Shared Loss Rate 40.00%
16 Earned Savings / Losses $30,856,352 $3,685,876 —
Revenue-to-Expense Category High Revenue

Population Segment BY1BY2BY3PY Trend Risk-Adj. Expected Difference

Total $13,602 $12,693 $13,238

Risk Scores

Hierarchical Condition Category risk scores. A score of 1.0 = national average. Below 1.0 means the population is healthier than average.

CMS-HCC Risk Scores

Population Segment BY1BY2BY3PY Trend Coding Gap MSSP Med.
End-Stage Renal Disease (ESRD) 1.076 1.011 1.076 1.055 → +0.095 0.967
Disabled 1.012 1.004 1.034 1.102 ↑ +0.163 0.934
Aged, Dual-Eligible 1.130 1.203 1.235 1.297 ↑ +0.266 0.954
Aged, Non-Dual 1.133 1.143 1.184 1.232 ↑ +0.228 0.983

Demographic Risk Scores

Demographic-only risk scores (age/sex/Medicaid status). Compared with HCC risk scores, the difference reflects clinical coding intensity.

Population Segment BY3PY
End-Stage Renal Disease (ESRD) 0.970 0.960
Disabled 0.939 0.939
Aged, Dual-Eligible 1.033 1.031
Aged, Non-Dual 1.006 1.004

Risk Redistribution Weights (PY)

Fraction of the ACO's total risk attributable to each population segment.

ESRD Weight 1.2%
Disabled Weight 3.8%
Aged Dual Weight 3.2%
Aged Non-Dual Weight 91.8%

Clinical Quality Measures

MSSP quality measures. Measures marked "lower is better" are inverted — a lower score indicates better performance.

Measure This ACO MSSP Median Peer Median
Overall Quality Score (Composite) p57 84.34 83.13 89.01
All-Cause Unplanned Admissions (risk-adjusted rate) (lower is better) ? p87 0.16 0.15 0.15

Peer Cohort

30 similar large, ENHANCED-track, above-average acuity, hospital-affiliated ACOs

View 30 peer ACOs
A2550A2272A3631A2729A3521A4976A3324A1535A4937A5331A3053A4575A2217A4938A1204A1175A1033A4931A1061A3380A2419A2024A1694A4949A3632A3473A2342A5176A4868A1001

Peers selected using weighted distance across 15 structural features (population acuity, composition, scale, care infrastructure, assignment type) within the ENHANCED track partition. Learn more

Methodology

Data source: MSSP ACO Performance PY2024 (dataset ID: mssp-performance). Vintage: PY2024. Downloaded: 2026-04-13. Rows in source: 476.

MSSP ACO Performance reports financial and quality performance for Accountable Care Organizations participating in the Medicare Shared Savings Program. Savings/losses are calculated against risk-adjusted, regionally-weighted benchmarks.

Full methodology →

MIT License. Free. No ads. No login.
GitHub · Report an error
